summary: Tested Molly & Sophie Shopify theme builder app (backend APIs + frontend Landing + /preview). All 9 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend landing page, all data-testids, download trigger, preview navigation, promo popup, cart drawer, free-shipping bar unlock at $50, valid/invalid promo codes all verified working. One UI bug found: clicking popup-copy throws an uncaught NotAllowedError because navigator.clipboard.writeText is denied in the iframe sandbox; the React dev error overlay then blocks subsequent interactions.

ui_bugs.issue: navigator.clipboard.writeText('WELCOME10') throws 'Write permission denied' (NotAllowedError) in cross-origin/iframe contexts. The error is uncaught (just `navigator.clipboard?.writeText('WELCOME10')` with optional chaining only guards against missing clipboard, NOT rejected promises). React dev overlay then covers the page and breaks all subsequent UI interactions. Wrap in try/catch or .catch(()=>{}), and add a fallback like document.execCommand('copy') or a hidden textarea selection.

Newsletter signup form on /preview just preventDefaults — never POSTs to /api/newsletter. The backend endpoint exists and works. Either wire the form to POST to ${API}/newsletter or document it as fully Klaviyo-side. Also no data-testid on the input/submit.

issues: Social icons render as plain 'IG/FB/TT' text circles with no links. Spec asked for instagram (shopmollyandsophie), facebook (mollyandsophie), tiktok (mollyandsophie.com). Add anchor tags with real handles.

action_items: Fix popup-copy: wrap navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch to swallow the iframe permission rejection; optionally fall back to a textarea+execCommand copy so the code actually copies inside the iframe.
Wire /preview newsletter form to POST to /api/newsletter (or remove pretense of working signup), and add data-testid attributes.
Replace placeholder IG/FB/TT footer chips with real social links to instagram.com/shopmollyandsophie, facebook.com/mollyandsophie, tiktok.com/@mollyandsophie.com.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses subprocess to invoke `zip` binary — fine but brittle if container lacks zip. Consider Python's zipfile module for portability.
_rebuild_zip is sync inside async route — for small theme it's fine, but blocks the event loop. Fine for now given size.
navigator.clipboard?.writeText() with optional chaining does NOT prevent promise rejection; this is the root cause of the uncaught runtime error overlay.
ThemePreview.jsx is 458 lines and single-component — could be split into Header/Hero/Cart/Popup subcomponents (>700-line rule not yet violated).

success_rate.frontend: ~92% (all critical flows verified; popup-copy throws uncaught error)

seed_data_creation: None - tests use unique uuid emails for contact/newsletter; MongoDB collections contact_messages and newsletter_signups will accumulate TEST_ entries.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all 5 API endpoints. Frontend landing page and /preview all data-testids present and verified. Re-test popup-copy after the clipboard fix is applied — the uncaught error must NOT surface; verify by clicking popup-copy and confirming no 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay shows up and the button text changes to '✓ Copied!'.
rca of the issue: popup-copy click bug RCA: In ThemePreview.jsx line 78, `navigator.clipboard?.writeText('WELCOME10')` returns a Promise. In sandboxed/iframe contexts (which this preview environment is) the Clipboard API rejects with NotAllowedError 'Write permission denied'. Optional chaining only protects against `navigator.clipboard` being undefined — it does NOT catch promise rejections. React dev mode then surfaces it as an uncaught runtime error overlay that covers the entire page, blocking all further interactions. Fix: `navigator.clipboard?.writeText('WELCOME10').catch(() => {})` or wrap in try/catch with a textarea+execCommand fallback for iframe copy.
